name: Mozarabic
notes: Mozarabic (8th–13th c. CE). Romance language of Al-Andalus, spoken by Christians, some Jews/Muslims under Islamic rule. Attested in kharjas (Arabic/Hebrew-script Romance stanzas) and botanical/medical texts. ARCHAIC features: no ĕ/ŏ diphthongisation (noite not noche), F- preserved as [f] (not [h]), Latin PL-/CL-/FL- preserved, -CT- → [jt] not [tʃ]. Extinct by ~13th century, replaced by advancing Castilian, Portuguese, and Catalan. Written in Arabic/Hebrew scripts.

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language
